Description: Warp uses composable Filter trait for routing, extraction, and response generation
Do: Chain filters with .and(); use warp::path for routes; use .or() for alternatives; use .recover() for error handling
Dont: Define routes imperatively; manually parse request components

Description: Use custom filters to inject shared state into handlers
Do: Create a with_db() filter using warp::any().map(move || state.clone()); pass state to each route
Dont: Use global mutable state; recreate resources per request

Description: Warp uses Rejection type for errors; use .recover() to map rejections to HTTP responses
Do: Define custom rejections; implement a recover filter; map to JSON error responses
Dont: Let warp return default HTML error pages; expose internal errors

Guideline: Use warp::log and custom filters for middleware
Description: Warp uses composable filters for middleware-like behavior
Do: Use warp::log for access logging; create filter chains for auth; use .with() for wrapping
Dont: Build middleware from scratch without using filter composition
Code Bad: let routes = api_routes;\n// no logging, no CORS\n// API will reject cross-origin requests

Description: Warp provides test utilities to test individual filters without network overhead
Do: Use warp::test::request() to build test requests; test filters in isolation; verify response bodies and status
Dont: Start a real HTTP server for testing
